headline: Dow, Nasdaq Turn Lower to Wrap Wild Week
summary: Sideways trading for stocks continued heading into the final hour of the week. The Dow was down 40 points, or 0.1%. It would be rising if not for steep drops in Goldman Sachs and Caterpillar, which are two of the largest stocks in the stock-price-weighted index.

raw.headline: Dow, Nasdaq Turn Lower to Wrap Wild Week
raw.summary: Sideways trading for stocks continued heading into the final hour of the week. The Dow was down 40 points, or 0.1%. It would be rising if not for steep drops in Goldman Sachs and Caterpillar, which are two of the largest stocks in the stock-price-weighted index.

headline: Nasdaq Rises. Caterpillar and Goldman Are Dragging on the Dow.
summary: Stocks were cruising along in Friday trading, and even the Nasdaq was in on the action.  The Dow rose 100 points, or 0.2%.  The index was rising, even though declines for Caterpillar and Goldman Sachs combined were shaving off more than 400 points on their own.

headline: Global Cold Feet on AI Trade Affect Pre-Markets
summary: This week of trading had so far been established early on: sinking in the Nasdaq and, by consequence, the S&P 500, while staying in the green for the blue-chip Dow and small-cap Russell 2000.

headline: Beyond Cloud & Memory: Infrastructure ETFs to Buy Amid AI Data Center Boom
summary: ETFs like PAVE offer diversified exposure to AI data center power infrastructure as utilities and grid equipment firms benefit from rising AI build-out spending.

headline: Caterpillar's Operating Margins Remain Under Pressure: Rebound Ahead?
summary: CAT's Q1 2026 margin slips to 18% as tariff-driven costs rise; $600M impact below estimates, with $2.2-$2.4B expected for 2026.

headline: How CAT Stock Doubles Again To Reach $2,000
summary: Caterpillar (NYSE: CAT) has undergone a historic structural re-rating over the past year. Closing at $1,057 on June 25, 2026, the stock has surged roughly 172% from its level of $388 just a year ago.
